acceptance_criteria.description: Portable skills describe affordance requirements, suggested primitive steps, decision points, compatible profiles, and success predicates without exposing opaque world commands.
A second game adopts the released WorldEval contracts and skill templates without changing the core protocol and saves an independently verified deterministic replay.
Cross-game evaluation reports authority-derived skill expansion, action validity, adaptation, efficiency, and replay verification metrics without an LLM judge.

compatibility: Skill additions remain compatible with worldeval-agent/0.1.0 or ship under an additive protocol version.
migration: Adopt skills one game at a time with conformance fixtures and replay evidence.
privacy: Skills and evaluation use declared affordances and player-visible evidence only.
rollback: Games continue accepting primitive plans when optional skill templates are absent.

summary: Prove that agent-side skills and evaluation primitives transfer across games without moving strategy into the game authority.
title: Reusable agent skills, evaluation, and additional games
